reason: I/O completion moves a blocked process to Ready; it must wait for the scheduler before running.
FCFS is non-preemptive, so it gives terrible response time where each user needs a regular CPU share: time-sharing systems.
CPU is not in memory so 'all of the mentioned' is out; the intended answer is the resident operating system, though user processes also occupy main memory, making this item ambiguous.
Device drivers hide device differences and expose a uniform interface to the I/O subsystem.
Stack-based IDS sits inside the TCP/IP stack and inspects packets as they traverse it.
The OS system-wide table of currently open files is called the open-file table.
Disk I/O is privileged, so a user process must issue a system call to the OS; the CPU has no system-call target.
Unix creates processes via fork().
FAT = File Allocation Table.
Banker's is strictly a deadlock-avoidance algorithm; with no 'avoidance' option, 'prevent deadlock' is the only choice that means deadlock never occurs (it is neither recovery nor resolution).
DSM = Distributed Shared Memory.
IDL = Interface Definition Language (CORBA/RPC stub generation).
The virtual address space size is bounded by the number of address lines (address bus width), not by installed RAM.
Windows 7 is system software (an OS); WordPad, Photoshop and Excel are applications.
MS-DOS is a single-tasking, single-user OS; Linux, Windows and macOS are multitasking.
FIFO/FCFS runs each job to completion without preemption.
An AND gate outputs HIGH only when every input is 1.
Only AND guarantees a LOW output whenever any input is 0; NOR outputs HIGH when all inputs are 0.
Standard TTL uses a nominal +5 V Vcc.
3-input AND is the product of all three inputs: X = ABC.
